<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]][[cdo:Bīng-ìng]][[de:Feuer-Tiger]][[fr:Tigre de feu]][[hak:Piáng-yìm]][[ja:丙寅]][[ko:병인]][[nds:Füür-Tiger]][[vi:Bính Dần]][[za:Bingjyinz]][[zh_min_nan:Péng-în]][[zh_yue:丙寅]][[zh:丙寅]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]][[cdo:Bīng-ìng]][[de:Feuer-Tiger]][[fr:Tigre de feu]][[hak:Piáng-yìm]][[ja:丙寅]][[ko:병인]][[nds:Füür-Tiger]][[vi:Bính Dần]][[za:Bingjyinz]][[zh_min_nan:Péng-în]][[zh_yue:丙寅]][[zh:丙寅]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]][[cdo:Bīng-ìng]][[de:Feuer-Tiger]][[fr:Tigre de feu]][[hak:Piáng-yìm]][[ja:丙寅]][[ko:병인]][[nds:Füür-Tiger]][[vi:Bính Dần]][[za:Bingjyinz]][[zh-min-nan:Péng-în]][[zh-yue:丙寅]][[zh:丙寅]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q767248]] Wikidata name: Yang Fire Tiger [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Fire Tiger|kanji=丙寅|romaji=hinoe tora|onyomi=heiin|p=bǐngyín|w=ping-yin|mi=pʰiŋ²¹ ȵiŋ²¹|y=bing2 jan4|j=bing2 jan4|ci=pɪŋ jɐn²|mc=pæŋ ŋin|oc-bs=praŋ ŋin|qq=}} markdown {{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Fire Tiger also known as Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]].
It is the third in the sequence of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]], following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|stem]] 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the [[:en:Earthly Branches|branch]] 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín ** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora ***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora ***Onyomi 1: Heiin ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần
<!-- == * Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín ** Cantonese (Jyutping): beng2 jan4 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: ひのえとら (hinoe-tora) ***Onyomi: へいかのとら (heika-no-tora) ***Onyomi: へいいん (heiin) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): byeongin ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần
--> ==Years of Yang Fire Tiger== A year is classified as Heiin if its [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Western calendar]] year, when divided by 60, leaves a remainder of 6. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Heiin Years''' |- ![[:en:1st millennium|1st millennium]]!![[:en:2nd millennium|2nd millennium]]!![[:en:3rd millennium|3rd millennium]] |- | *[[:en:6|6]] *[[:en:66|66]] *[[:en:126|126]] *[[:en:186|186]] *[[:en:246|246]] *[[:en:306|306]] *[[:en:366|366]] *[[:en:426|426]] *[[:en:486|486]] *[[:en:546|546]] *[[:en:606|606]] *[[:en:666|666]] *[[:en:726|726]] *[[:en:786|786]] *[[:en:846|846]] *[[:en:906|906]] *[[:en:966|966]] | *[[:en:1026|1026]] *[[:en:1086|1086]] *[[:en:1146|1146]] *[[:en:1206|1206]] *[[:en:1266|1266]] *[[:en:1326|1326]] *[[:en:1386|1386]] *[[:en:1446|1446]] *[[:en:1506|1506]] *[[:en:1566|1566]] *[[:en:1626|1626]] *[[:en:1686|1686]] *[[:en:1746|1746]] *[[:en:1806|1806]] *[[:en:1866|1866]] *[[:en:1926|1926]] *[[:en:1986|1986]] | *[[:en:2046|2046]] *[[:en:2106|2106]] *[[:en:2166|2166]] *[[:en:2226|2226]] *[[:en:2286|2286]] *[[:en:2346|2346]] *[[:en:2406|2406]] *[[:en:2466|2466]] *[[2526]] *[[2586]] *[[2646]] *[[:en:2706|2706]] *[[2766]] *[[2826]] *[[2886]] *[[:en:2946|2946]] |}
==Months of Yang Fire Tiger== The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|stem]] is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
==Days of Yang Fire Tiger== === Rekichū (Lower Section) === Heiin days in the [[Lower Section rekichū]] are as follows: * [[Unlucky Meeting Days]] (for the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar]] months of 3 and 9) == See Also == *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:Pêng-chún]] [[zh-yue:丙寅]]
{{translated page|ja|丙寅|qq=Yang Fire Tiger|version=98658211|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[cdo:Bîng-cṳ̄de]] [[nds:Holt-Tigeri]] [[de:Yang Fire Tiger]]
[[ja:丙寅]] [[zh:丙寅]] [[ko:병인]] [[fr:Tigre de feu]] [[de:Feuer-Tiger]] [[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]] [[de:Feuer-Tigreen]] [[en:Fire Tiger]] [[fr:Tigre de feushak]] [[it:Bing-chṳ́id]]
[[vi:Bính Dần]] [[za:Bingceij]] [[ar:Yang Fire Tiger]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]][[cdo:Bīng-ìng]][[de:Feuer-Tiger]][[fr:Tigre de feu]][[hak:Piáng-yìm]][[ja:丙寅]][[ko:병인]][[nds:Füür-Tiger]][[vi:Bính Dần]][[za:Bingjyinz]][[zh-min-nan:Péng-în]][[zh-yue:丙寅]][[zh:丙寅]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]][[cdo:Bīng-ìng]][[de:Feuer-Tiger]][[fr:Tigre de feu]][[hak:Piáng-yìm]][[ja:丙寅]][[ko:병인]][[nds:Füür-Tiger]][[vi:Bính Dần]][[za:Bingjyinz]][[zh-min-nan:Péng-în]][[zh-yue:丙寅]][[zh:丙寅]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Vuurtier (astrologie)]][[cdo:Bīng-ìng]][[de:Feuer-Tiger]][[fr:Tigre de feu]][[hak:Piáng-yìm]][[ja:丙寅]][[ko:병인]][[nds:Füür-Tiger]][[vi:Bính Dần]][[za:Bingjyinz]][[zh-min-nan:Péng-în]][[zh-yue:丙寅]][[zh:丙寅]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q767248]] Wikidata name: Yang Fire Tiger [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Chinese|l=Yang Fire Tiger|kanji=丙寅|romaji=hinoe tora|onyomi=heiin|p=bǐngyín|w=ping-yin|mi=pʰiŋ²¹ ȵiŋ²¹|y=bing2 jan4|j=bing2 jan4|ci=pɪŋ jɐn²|mc=pæŋ ŋin|oc-bs=praŋ ŋin|qq=}} markdown {{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}} '''Yang Fire Tiger also known as Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]].
It is the third in the sequence of the [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|Sexagenary cycle]], following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|stem]] 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the [[:en:Earthly Branches|branch]] 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.
==Pronunciation== * English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín ** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora ***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora ***Onyomi 1: Heiin ** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인) ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần
<!-- == * Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) ** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín ** Cantonese (Jyutping): beng2 jan4 ** Japanese: ***Kunyomi: ひのえとら (hinoe-tora) ***Onyomi: へいかのとら (heika-no-tora) ***Onyomi: へいいん (heiin) ** Korean (Revised Romanization): byeongin ** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần
--> ==Years of Yang Fire Tiger== A year is classified as Heiin if its [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Western calendar]] year, when divided by 60, leaves a remainder of 6. {| class="wikitable" |+'''Heiin Years''' |- ![[:en:1st millennium|1st millennium]]!![[:en:2nd millennium|2nd millennium]]!![[:en:3rd millennium|3rd millennium]] |- | *[[:en:6|6]] *[[:en:66|66]] *[[:en:126|126]] *[[:en:186|186]] *[[:en:246|246]] *[[:en:306|306]] *[[:en:366|366]] *[[:en:426|426]] *[[:en:486|486]] *[[:en:546|546]] *[[:en:606|606]] *[[:en:666|666]] *[[:en:726|726]] *[[:en:786|786]] *[[:en:846|846]] *[[:en:906|906]] *[[:en:966|966]] | *[[:en:1026|1026]] *[[:en:1086|1086]] *[[:en:1146|1146]] *[[:en:1206|1206]] *[[:en:1266|1266]] *[[:en:1326|1326]] *[[:en:1386|1386]] *[[:en:1446|1446]] *[[:en:1506|1506]] *[[:en:1566|1566]] *[[:en:1626|1626]] *[[:en:1686|1686]] *[[:en:1746|1746]] *[[:en:1806|1806]] *[[:en:1866|1866]] *[[:en:1926|1926]] *[[:en:1986|1986]] | *[[:en:2046|2046]] *[[:en:2106|2106]] *[[:en:2166|2166]] *[[:en:2226|2226]] *[[:en:2286|2286]] *[[:en:2346|2346]] *[[:en:2406|2406]] *[[:en:2466|2466]] *[[2526]] *[[2586]] *[[2646]] *[[:en:2706|2706]] *[[2766]] *[[2826]] *[[2886]] *[[:en:2946|2946]] |}
==Months of Yang Fire Tiger== The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|stem]] is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
==Days of Yang Fire Tiger== === Rekichū (Lower Section) === Heiin days in the [[Lower Section rekichū]] are as follows: * [[Unlucky Meeting Days]] (for the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar]] months of 3 and 9) == See Also == *{{prefix|qq=}} *{{intitle|qq=}}

{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yang Fire Tiger also known as  Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}.

It is the third in the sequence of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}}, following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem}} 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=branch}} 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora
***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora
***Onyomi 1: Heiin
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

<!--
==
* Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) 
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): beng2 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi: ひのえとら (hinoe-tora)
***Onyomi: へいかのとら (heika-no-tora)
***Onyomi: へいいん (heiin)
** Korean (Revised Romanization): byeongin
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

-->
==Years of Yang Fire Tiger==
A year is classified as Heiin if its {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Western calendar}} year, when divided by 60, leaves a remainder of 6.

==Months of Yang Fire Tiger==
The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem}} is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu}}.

==Days of Yang Fire Tiger==
=== Rekichū (Lower Section) ===
Heiin days in the [[Lower Section rekichū]] are as follows:
* [[Unlucky Meeting Days]] (for the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar]] months of 3 and 9)

markdown
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yang Fire Tiger also known as  Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}.

It is the third in the sequence of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}}, following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem}} 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=branch}} 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora
***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora
***Onyomi 1: Heiin
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

<!--
==
* Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) 
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): beng2 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi: ひのえとら (hinoe-tora)
***Onyomi: へいかのとら (heika-no-tora)
***Onyomi: へいいん (heiin)
** Korean (Revised Romanization): byeongin
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

-->
==Years of Yang Fire Tiger==
A year is classified as Heiin if its {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Western calendar}} year, when divided by 60, leaves a remainder of 6.

==Months of Yang Fire Tiger==
The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem}} is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu}}.

==Days of Yang Fire Tiger==
=== Rekichū (Lower Section) ===
Heiin days in the [[Lower Section rekichū]] are as follows:
* [[Unlucky Meeting Days]] (for the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar]] months of 3 and 9)

==Months of Yang Fire Tiger==
The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem}} is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu}}.

==Days of Yang Fire Tiger==
=== Rekichū (Lower Section) ===
Heiin days in the [[Lower Section rekichū]] are as follows:
* [[Unlucky Meeting Days]] (for the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar]] months of 3 and 9)

markdown
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yang Fire Tiger also known as  Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}.

It is the third in the sequence of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle}}, following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem}} 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=branch}} 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora
***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora
***Onyomi 1: Heiin
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

<!--
==
* Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) 
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): beng2 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi: ひのえとら (hinoe-tora)
***Onyomi: へいかのとら (heika-no-tora)
***Onyomi: へいいん (heiin)
** Korean (Revised Romanization): byeongin
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

-->

markdown
{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yang Fire Tiger also known as  Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}}.

It is the third in the sequence of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle|1=Sexagenary cycle}}, following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem|1=Heavenly Stems}} 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=branch|1=Earthly Branches}} 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora
***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora
***Onyomi 1: Heiin
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

<!--
==
* Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅) 
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): beng2 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi: ひのえとら (hinoe-tora)
***Onyomi: へいかのとら (heika-no-tora)
***Onyomi: へいいん (heiin)
** Korean (Revised Romanization): byeongin
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

-->
==Years of Yang Fire Tiger==
A year is classified as Heiin if its {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Western calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} year, when divided by 60, leaves a remainder of 6.

==Months of Yang Fire Tiger==
The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem|1=Heavenly Stems}} is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu|1=Jingzhe}}.

==Days of Yang Fire Tiger==
=== Rekichū (Lower Section) ===
Heiin days in the [[Lower Section rekichū]] are as follows:
* [[Unlucky Meeting Days]] (for the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar]] months of 3 and 9)

{{Sexagenary cycle|qq=}}
'''Yang Fire Tiger also known as  Heiin''' (丙寅, ひのえとら, へいかのとら, へいいん) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle|stems-and-branches]] combinations in the traditional {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}}.

It is the third in the sequence of the {{ill|Sexagenary cycle|en|Sexagenary cycle|WD=Q246658|lt=Sexagenary cycle|1=Sexagenary cycle}}, following [[乙丑|Otsuchū]] and preceding [[丁卯|Teibō]]. In the [[Yin-yang and the Five Elements|Yin-Yang and Five Elements theory]], the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem|1=Heavenly Stems}} 丙 represents Yang and Fire, while the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=branch|1=Earthly Branches}} 寅 represents Yang and Wood, forming a [[Five Elements#Mutual Generation|mutual generation (Wood generates Fire)]] relationship.

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yang Fire Tiger (丙寅)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): bǐngyín
** Cantonese (Jyutping): bing3 jan4
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Hinoe-tora
***Kunyomi 2: Heika-no-tora
***Onyomi 1: Heiin
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): byeongin (병인)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Bính Dần

==Years of Yang Fire Tiger==
A year is classified as Heiin if its {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Western calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} year, when divided by 60, leaves a remainder of 6.

==Months of Yang Fire Tiger==
The first month (January) of a year ending in 4 or 9 in the Western calendar (when the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=stem|1=Heavenly Stems}} is [[:en:甲|Kō]] or [[:en:己|Ki]]) is designated as a Heiin month. However, this is based on the [[:en:Chinese lunar calendar|lunar calendar]] or the [[solar term calendar]], where the month may start from [[:en:Lìchūn|Risshun]] to the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu|1=Jingzhe}}.
